title: quercus × andresii
intro: Quercus × andresii, also known as the hybrid white oak, is a rare and lesser-known species of oak tree that is native to the southwestern United States. The bark and leaves of Quercus × andresii contain tannins, which have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The medicinal properties of Quercus × andresii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against infections, making it beneficial for relieving symptoms of the common cold and flu. Herbal preparations of Quercus × andresii can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its health benefits.
